steps.title: Set up the discriminant condition for two solutions
steps.content:
Shortcut (discriminant): For $88x^2 + bx + c = 0$ to have more than one real solution, the discriminant must be positive: $b^2 - 4ac > 0$. With $a = 88$:
$b^2 - 4(88)c > 0$
$b^2 - 352c > 0$
$b^2 > 352c$

steps.title: Solve for b
steps.content: Since $b$ is positive, take the square root: $b > \sqrt{352c}$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
